S225 GR: REM S226 Material usage: REM S227 Product costs: REM Information Structures Information Structure Descriptions Table 20.4 Standard Information Structures Available for PP (Cont.) © 1039 Chapter 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning Timely, accurate, and comprehensive information helps companies make better and faster business decisions. A large number of SAP Fiori apps are available not just for production planning but also for logistics and supply chain management, and they can be used immediately. SAP Fiori is the next-generation web-based user experience for working with the SAP S/4HANA system, and each app is accessed via a tile in the SAP Fiori launchpad. A large number of SAP Fiori apps are available in SAP S/4HANA, and the SAP Fiori apps refer- ence library is constantly expanding. Apps for master data, transactional data, report- ing, and analytics in all SAP areas are available in the SAP Fiori apps reference library. We’ll cover some of them in this chapter, after taking a quick look at the SAP Fiori launchpad and the library for all SAP Fiori apps. 21.1 SAP Fiori Launchpad and SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library Figure 21.1 shows the initial screen of the SAP Fiori launchpad, wherein individual apps are represented as tiles. The apps can be grouped together based on different roles, such as materials planner, production planner, and inventory controller. They can also be grouped together based on functional areas such as production planning (PP), mate- rials management (MM), and quality management (QM), as shown in the different tabs in Figure 21.1. Due to their highly intuitive nature, the apps make it easy to quickly get up to speed. Furthermore, most master data and transactional data apps and even sev- eral reporting apps in SAP Fiori still give the look and feel of the SAP GUI by showing the same menu options, menu bars, buttons, and icons. The top-right-hand corner of the SAP Fiori launchpad provides an option to search for an app by its name or by transac- tion code, such as COR2 (change process order). The library of SAP Fiori apps is constantly expanding at a rapid pace for both SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. To search for and explore the latest or most rele- vant SAP Fiori apps, go to http://s-prs.co/v216700 and click the Home icon. On the initial screen of the SAP Fiori apps reference library, you can search for a relevant app on the left-hand side of the screen and view app details on the right-hand side. To make rele- vant apps available for use, reach out to your SAP Fiori consultant with an app’s details and the specific role or group to which it needs to be assigned. 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1040 Figure 21.1 SAP Fiori Launchpad Homepage 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications In the following sections, we’ll cover some of the SAP Fiori apps of PP, such as Monitor Material Coverage, Monitor External and Internal Requirements, and Manage Work Center Capacity. 21.2.1 Monitor Material Coverage App This app enables a production planner to monitor all the materials in a selected area of responsibility. This includes MTS and collective requirements materials, MTO and ETO materials, and materials for direct production and direct procurement. The app also provides a list of all materials with a net requirement segment or individual segments. These materials might have coverage issues according to a specified shortage defini- tion, and the shortage definition a planner chooses contains a set of rules that the sys- tem uses for calculating the material shortages. These rules define the scope of the shortage calculation—that is, the supply and demand elements to be considered and the conditions that have to be met for a shortage to be relevant. Figure 21.2 shows the Monitor Material Coverage app. In it the green bars represent materials for which there is a comfortable stock availability situation. The red bars rep- resent a situation that the inventory or materials planner needs to pay greater atten- tion to in order to avoid stock-outs or unavailability of required material on the desired date. The displayed columns show the shortage quantity and when the first shortage is likely to happen, and more columns can be displayed too. Multiple dropdowns on the left-hand side of the screen provide options for the materials or production planner to filter materials based on different criteria. After applying a new filter, the planner can © 1041 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications click Go and then use the default Time till Shortage, Material, and Individual Segment filters to narrow down the scope of the list. Figure 21.2 Monitor Material Coverage App 21.2.2 Monitor External Requirements App To access the Monitor External Requirements app, you click a material code on the screen shown in Figure 21.2—for example, 2269 —and the screen shown in Figure 21.3 appears. The app offers monitoring of the uncovered requirements originating from sales orders and stock transport orders in a selected area of responsibility. A planner can use the default Time till Requirement Date filter to determine the period in which they wants to check for uncovered external requirements. Only one shortage defini- tion is available for calculating uncovered requirements, and it is displayed for infor- mation purposes. This app provides necessary information that helps materials plan- ners react to shortage situations and fulfill customers’ demands on time. Figure 21.3 Monitor External Requirements App Figure 21.4 shows a detailed view of Material 2269’s stock or shortage situation and the time (in days) it will take to replenish the stock of the material. Next, choose Open  Manage Material Coverage in the bottom right hand corner of Figure 21.4 and the screen shown in Figure 21.5 will appear. 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1042 Figure 21.4 Details of Individual Material Coverage Tips and Tricks You can also directly navigate to the Manage External Requirements app (see Figure 21.5) to view details of the selected delayed items. You can also check different solu- tions by selecting an item on the screen in Figure 21.3 and clicking Manage Item in the bottom-right-hand corner of the screen (not shown). Figure 21.5 Manage External Requirements © 1043 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications The screen shown in Figure 21.5 is similar to the stock/requirements list (Transaction MD04), but it offers more features, including the ability to run MRP interactively (see Start MRP Run in the bottom-right-hand corner of the screen). The Material Informa- tion tab shows the detailed material planning settings for a material. 21.2.3 Monitor Internal Requirements App With the Monitor Internal Requirements app, you can ensure that components are available in the right quantities and in time to fulfill internal requirements originating from production orders, process orders, maintenance orders, and network orders. To help you determine which receipts and requirements are included in the coverage cal- culation, a shortage definition is available and displayed in the app. The app provides all the necessary information to help you react quickly to critical situations. From this app, you can directly navigate to the Manage Internal Requirements app to view details of the selected materials and check different solutions for shortages. Choose any mate- rial on the screen shown in Figure 21.6 to view the screen shown in Figure 21.7. Figure 21.6 Monitor Internal Requirements App The screen shown in Figure 21.7 has the look and feel of the stock/requirements list (Transaction MD04). The bottom-right-hand corner of the screen offers the option to trigger the Start MRP Run app (not shown), which will then plan the material again. You can also use the Create Order option to create procurement elements, such as a planned order for in-house production or a purchase requisition for external procure- ment, as long as a source of supply is defined for the material. You can also choose to edit an order, covert a planned order into a production or process order, or even delete a planned order (PlnOrd). 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1044 Figure 21.7 Manage Material Coverage App, Detailed Screen 21.2.4 Manage Production Orders App With the Manage Production Orders app, you can manage those production orders that have issues based on the settings made in the Monitor Production Orders app. A plan- ner can view details regarding delayed materials and missing components, as well as the status of operations and milestones. This app provides all the necessary informa- tion for planners to react quickly to critical situations and avoid delays in production. The Issues column shown in Figure 21.8 includes icons in each row for four important categories: material shortage, scheduling delay, quality issues, and material delay. For each category, if there’s an issue, the related icon turns red. In this situation, you select an item and then choose Check Availability—for example, to view the number of missing components required to produce a product. Choosing a production order and then clicking the Details button (the forward arrow > located on the far-right-hand side of the screen) will bring up a detailed screen that you can used not only to evaluate the current status of an order but also to perform several important business functions. Some of the functions are changing an order, confirming an order, confirming an oper- ation, and setting an order to the technically complete status. The mini charts at the top of the screen provide a summary of the status of various process orders, and you can click them to get greater insights into the issues. This is shown in Figure 21.9. Figure 21.9 shows the detailed screen of the Manage Production Order app. Notice the same issues that were previously shown in the overview screen. Here again, a user can scroll down and not just evaluate the details of a production order (such as compo- nents, operations, and order schedule) but also perform business functions such as confirming a production order. (Various tabs in Figure 21.9 facilitate this.) © 1045 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications Figure 21.8 Manage Production Order App Figure 21.9 Manage Production Order App, Detailed Screen 21.2.5 Display MRP Master Data Issues App The Display MRP Master Data Issues app provides details of the materials in a specific plant for which an issue occurred in the last MRP run. You can display both types of information—on materials for which messages were created in the MRP run and on those that may not have been planned at all. Information about the responsible MRP controller and the date and time that the last planning run was carried out is also displayed. After you address the issues faced during materials planning, you can plan 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1046 materials correctly in future planning runs. Figure 21.10 shows the overview of the issues faced in planning for several materials. Figure 21.10 Display MRP Master Data Issues App 21.2.6 Scrap Reason App The Scrap Reason app alerts users based on KPIs and enables a step-by-step data analy- sis from different perspectives to identify the root causes of scrap in the production process. The app shows information on the distribution of actual scrap recorded in pro- duction confirmations in the dimensions of time, work center, material, plant, and rea- son for variance. As a production planner, your ability to analyze the confirmed scrap and rework makes it possible for you to work on the reasons or root causes of the great- est loss of production, which will help you prevent problems in the future (see Figure 21.11). You should be sure to check out more display options from the Yield, Scrap, and Rework % by Entry Time (Line Chart) dropdown list. You can also change the chart types—for example, from the horizontal bars shown to a pie chart—by selecting one of the chart options available in the top right-hand corner of the screen. You can also dis- play the data in table form. © 1047 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications Figure 21.11 Scrap Reason App 21.2.7 Operation Scrap App Figure 21.12 shows the Operation Scrap app, which enables a planner to compare actual scrap figures recorded in the production confirmations with the expected scrap that was defined in the order operation. It allows the planner to implement a feedback loop for updating the expected scrap percentage in the order operation. The app shows the work centers where the expected scrap percentage most differs from the actual scrap percentage. Figure 21.12 Operations Scrap App 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1048 21.2.8 Reprocess Failed Material Movements App The Reprocess Failed Material Movements app enables you to view and process unsuc- cessful materials movements, and it also provides the reasons why these goods move- ments failed. From this app, you can navigate to the root cause of the problem, such as a missing storage location or an incorrect posting period, and then correct the problem. To correct a failed material movement issue, you select a material and choose Reprocess. Figure 21.13 shows the SAP Fiori version of Transaction COGI (Reprocess Failed Material Movements), which we covered in Chapter 6, Section 6.9.1. Here, it shows that a few goods movements failed due to one issue or another. Figure 21.13 Reprocess Failed Material Movements App 21.2.9 Manage Work Center Capacity App With the Manage Work Center Capacity app (see Figure 21.14), a planner can visualize the capacities of work centers and the load on work centers. The app allows a planner to detect overload situations, along with the cumulative overload situation across the selected evaluation horizons based on bucket (or grouping) types, such as days or weeks. For a work center, several factors have to be balanced to ensure an optimal utili- zation of capacity such that there are no overloads across the evaluation horizon, as well as no backlog in terms of orders or operations. With the information provided in the app, a planner can make informed decisions to achieve this balance. It’s also possible to evaluate critical and overload situations based on priority, using KPIs such as first critical load, first overload, and maximum load. These critical and © 1049 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications normal load levels can be personalized as per the requirement that anything above critical will be considered an overload. Figure 21.14 Manage Work Center Capacity App With the app, a planner can view the capacity required to fulfill all the open orders, such as planned, production, and process orders, in a set evaluation horizon. The planner chooses a work center and clicks the forward arrow (>) located on far right-hand side of the screen, and the screen shown in Figure 21.15 appears. Figure 21.15 Work Center Capacity 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1050 With the Manage Work Center Capacity app, a planner can get details of the capacity sit- uation a of work center and the loads on the work center. The app offers the ability to detect overload situations across select evaluation horizons based on bucket (or group- ing) types, such as days or weeks. For a work center, a planner attempts to balance sev- eral factors to ensure an optimal utilization of capacity so that there are no overloads across the evaluation horizon, as well as no backlog in terms of orders or operations. With the information provided in the app, a planner can make informed decisions to achieve this balance (see Figure 21.15). 21.2.10 Material Documents Overview App Figure 21.16 shows the Material Documents Overview app, which has a look and feel that’s similar to SAP GUI Transaction MB51. The top half of the figure offers several fil- ters that can be used to display only the required information, as shown in the lower half of the screen. You can choose a material document and click the forward arrow (>) located on far right-hand side of the screen, and the screen shown in Figure 21.17 will appear. Figure 21.16 Material Documents Overview App The screen shown in Figure 21.17 not only provides details of a material document but also provides the option for users to reverse it, via the Reverse option located in the top-right-hand corner. Clicking the Attachment tab (or scrolling down on the same screen) will enable a user to attach any related document, such as a quality certificate or a delivery document, to this material document. © 1051 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications Figure 21.17 Material Document 21.2.11 Manage Production Version App A production version is the link between the BOM of a product and its routing, master recipe, or rate routing. It determines which BOM relates to the relevant routing or mas- ter recipe to produce a material or plan a material. There may be different production versions based on the lot sizes and validity dates. With the Manage Production Version app, a user can create, edit, copy, and delete various production versions. Figure 21.18 shows the SAP Fiori version of the Transaction C223 (Manage Production Version) screen covered in Chapter 6, Section 6.2.5. Figure 21.18 Manage Production Version App 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1052 21.2.12 Capacity Scheduling Board App With the Capacity Scheduling Board app, a production planner can preschedule the orders to be dispatched from a work center based on their priority. A planner can select the source of supply (in the production version) and the work centers to dispatch the orders on the chosen dates. For ease of selection, the app highlights a work center with a capacity duration that meets the capacity required to complete the operation. The app also uses midpoint scheduling to adjust the operational time (of dispatch, resched- ule, etc.), according to the strategy that has been set. The production versions can be switched when the work center capacity is insufficient, and orders can be rescheduled if they overlap in a time slot (see Figure 21.19). Figure 21.19 Capacity Scheduling Board 21.2.13 Kanban Board App The Kanban Board app that we discussed in detail in Chapter 9 is a tool that a planner uses to monitor circulating kanban containers and evaluations, and it provides an overview of work progress and material consumption. The Kanban board presents a clear picture of missing part situations and bottlenecks, for example, for every supply area. For this purpose, the statuses of the kanban containers and the error situations are represented by different colors. The Kanban board can also be used to trigger the Kanban signal (see Figure 21.20). © 1053 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications Figure 21.20 Kanban Board App 21.2.14 Stock—Single and Multiple Materials App With the Stock—Single Material app, a planner and an inventory controller can get an overview of the material stock. Stock can be reviewed by the plants and storage loca- tions for which a planner is responsible, and the app can display the stock information as a table or a diagram. The app also features a comparison of the stock information with the safety stock and minimum safety stock values defined for the material. Furthermore, the detailed stock history for each plant or storage location for the last 11 periods (at period-end closing) and the stock balance at the reporting date can be viewed simply by clicking the graph located in the lower-right-hand corner of the screen (see Figure 21.21). Figure 21.21 Stock—Single Material App 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1054 You can switch to the graphical representation (see Figure 21.22) by clicking the Chart View icon located in the top-right-hand corner of the screen. This lets you not only view various stock types but also perform the desired transactions from the options located at the bottom of the screen. Figure 21.22 Graphical View of Stock—Single Material App Note You must meet the prerequisites listed in SAP Notes 2404856 and 2694741 before you can use either of these apps. Planners can use the Stock—Multiple Materials app to get an overview of material stocks. Planners can review stock for the plants and storage locations for which they or an inventory controller may be responsible. The app can also display the stock infor- mation as a table. The app calculates the stock values based on the stock quantity of the selected report- ing date multiplied by the current material price. This means that the app always makes its calculations using the current material price, irrespective of the reporting date that was selected. The result of a calculation method can be displayed based on the historical prices at the time of the related material postings. SAP recommends using the main calculation method based on the current material price, because this method leads to faster results and is optimized for stock analysis (see Figure 21.23). © 1055 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications Figure 21.23 Stock—Multiple Materials App 21.2.15 Manage Production Operations App With the Manage Production Operations app, a planner can monitor the progress of production and also get an overview of the current situation, with all the information needed to solve any issues. If, for example, a particular operation is delayed, then a planner can navigate to the details (see Figure 21.25) to see at a glance why the operation is delayed: perhaps there are missing components, the resource is occupied by another operation, or the previous operation hasn’t finished. All the information the planner needs is available so they can take immediate action to resolve the issue and keep the production processes running as smoothly as possible (see Figure 21.24). Figure 21.25 not only shows the issues at hand but also enables a planner to perform various business functions, such as making any date changes or confirming an opera- tion. (Refer to the various tabs in Figure 21.25, which also can be accessed simply by scrolling down in the same screen.) Figure 21.24 Manage Production Operations App 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1056 Figure 21.25 Manage Production Operations, Detailed View 21.2.16 Releasing Production Orders Apart from individually or collectively releasing production orders, it’s also possible to schedule their release based on the defined frequency and specify the parameters that the system will look for while releasing production orders. After you open the Scheduled Release Order Run app, an overview screen appears that displays the job runs of the past (see Figure 21.26). Figure 21.26 Releasing Production Order © 1057 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications Choose Create (not shown) to start an application job for making the necessary set- tings. The Job Template field 1 in Figure 21.26 is filled with a preselected option, while you can maintain a user-defined Job Name to clearly distinguish the individual appli- cation jobs. Scroll down and then check the Start Immediately box 2 to begin the appli- cation job right away. To schedule an application job, choose Define Recurrence Pat- tern. When scheduling a new job, it’s important to first define the scheduling options that will set the job start date and the recurrence pattern. Then. choose Step 3 on the screen in Figure 21.26 and the screen in Figure 21.27 will appear. Figure 21.27 Release Production Order, Scheduled Job After the scheduling step, the next step is to define the Selection Criteria settings that will be used to select which products will be classified in the job. Figure 21.27 shows some entries for the relevant plant as selection criteria, so the application job that meets these criteria will be scheduled for releasing production orders based on the defined frequency. On this screen, you can create a new job, copy an existing job, or check the results of the finished jobs. 21.2.17 Analyze PIR Quality App With the Analyze PIR Quality app, a planner can analyze how accurately the forecast matches the planned independent requirements (PIRs) for materials. The planner can also determine how well the forecasted demands for sales orders matched the quanti- ties that were actually delivered to customers. They can also analyze this data for monthly periods in the past. 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1058 The planner can simply click the fields in the Available Fields area shown in Figure 21.28 or even drag and drop them as rows or columns to create the necessary hierarchy to view the results. Not only can the planner filter or sort the data, but they can perform a large number of business functions, as shown in the Tools dropdown. If the displayed data has a single unit of measure, then the planner can visually present the same by choosing the Graphical Display option. Figure 21.28 Forecast Demand Accuracy 21.2.18 Excess Component Consumption App The Excess Component Consumption app provides a comparison of the expected com- ponent scrap percentage defined in the BOM item with the actual excess consumption recorded in the production confirmation. The problems that created the largest excess component consumption in the past can be analyzed, and a means to prevent these problems in the future can be suggested (see Figure 21.29). Figure 21.29 Excess Component Consumption App © 1059 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications 21.2.19 Analyze Costs by Work Center/Operation App The Analyze Costs by Work Center/Operation app provides reports on the cost infor- mation at the work center or operation level to help a planner analyze overall produc- tion costs. Figure 21.30 shows the graphical version of various costs at the work center and cost center and activity levels in the local or company code currencies. Clicking any of the mini graphs will bring up additional relevant cost-related details of the selection made. Figure 21.30 Analyze Costs by Work Center/Operation App 21.2.20 Production Cost Analysis App The Production Cost Analysis app provides reports on the cost information at the indi- vidual production order level to help a planner analyze overall production costs. Figure 21.31 shows the tabular version of various costs of individual production orders in the local or company code currencies. Figure 21.31 Production Cost Analysis App 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1060 You can choose Settings to display or hide the desired fields, and you can choose the forward arrow located on the far right-hand side of the screen to bring up more cost- and quantity-related details of the individual production order. 21.2.21 Capacity Scheduling Table App With the Capacity Scheduling Table app shown in Figure 21.32, you as a planner can pre- schedule orders to be dispatched from a pacemaker work center based on their priority. You can also select the source of supply (in the production version) and the work cen- ters to dispatch the orders on the dates that you choose. For ease of selection, the app highlights a work center with a capacity duration that meets the capacity required to complete the operation. The app also uses midpoint scheduling to adjust the opera- tional time (of dispatch, reschedule, and so on), according to the strategy that you have set. You can switch production versions when the work center capacity is insufficient or reschedule orders that coincide in a time slot. Figure 21.32 Capacity Scheduling Table App Some additional things you can do with this app are as follows:  Control important scheduling parameters, such as planning mode, scheduling strat- egy (finite or infinite scheduling) and direction of orders.  View capacity requirement using colors (red, green, and yellow) to determine whether work center capacity will suffice.  Set the maximum number of days (up to 30 days) possible to schedule orders in the past.  Execute mass dispatch or deallocation of order operations.  Filter and resolve late or overdue orders.  Realign scheduled orders to a past or a future date and time. © 1061 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications  Navigate to the Variant Configuration screen (in the advanced variant configura- tion) to view the product configuration for the production orders.  Reschedule or deallocate a scheduled operation on a different date.  Firm or unfirm deallocated planned orders.  View the requirement date based on dynamic pegging.  Change the source of supply if there is insufficient capacity to dispatch the order.  View time slots that have sufficient capacity to dispatch orders or edit slots (in dis- patch by sequence planning mode) that have overlapping order operation capacity.  Change the sequence of order in the sequence table by using the drag-and-drop fea- ture. 21.2.22 Manage Process Orders App In the Manage Process Orders app, shown in Figure 21.33, you as a planner can monitor and manage the progress of process orders. The app provides an overview of the cur- rent situation with all the information needed to solve any issues. If, for example, a par- ticular order is delayed, you can navigate to the details to see at a glance why the order is delayed—perhaps there are missing components or there is a quality deviation. You then have the information you need to take immediate action to resolve the issue and keep your production processes running as smoothly as possible. Figure 21.33 Manage Process Orders App Be sure to navigate to the detailed screen of a process order by choosing the forward arrow located on the far right-hand side of the screen. It contains very comprehensive and detailed information about the process order. 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1062 21.2.23 Manage Process Order Operations App Figure 21.34 shows the initial screen of the Manage Process Order Operations app. With this app, a planner can monitor the progress of process orders at the detailed opera- tions level. The app provides an overview of the phases, components, relationships between phases, and other operations linked to the process order. If a particular opera- tion of a process order is delayed, then the planner can identify the reason for the delayed operation. For example, there may be missing components, the resource may be occupied by another operation, or the previous operation may still not be finished. This visibility at the operation or phase level provides insights that a planner can use to take immediate action to resolve the issue and keep the operations running smoothly. Figure 21.34 Manage Process Order Operations App Next, click the forward arrow icon located on the far-right-hand side of the process order on the screen shown in Figure 21.34 to go to the screen shown in Figure 21.35. Figure 21.35 Operations Details © 1063 21.2 SAP Fiori Applications There, you can see details related to confirmation of the processing status of process orders, quality inspections of materials, and goods movement. Some additional things you can do with this app are as follows:  View the details of all the operations involved in a process order.  Determine whether the operation has been released, is planned, or is in progress.  View the progress of operations expressed as a percentage and the number of con- firmed phases.  Filter operations using adapt filters and additional visual filters, such as processing status, scheduled start date, component availability status, and quality status.  Export the entire list of orders and operations into a spreadsheet.  Determine whether the order has issues owing to time delays, missing components, or quality. An order can have quality issues for one of the following reasons:  Confirmed scrap quantity is greater than planned scrap quantity  Inspection lot characteristics are rejected  A usage decision is rejected 21.2.24 Production Execution Duration App With the Production Execution Duration app shown in Figure 21.36, you as a planner can compare the planned and actual operation durations required for execution. Figure 21.36 Production Execution Duration App 21 SAP Fiori Apps for Production Planning 1064 The planned operation durations are defined in the manufacturing order and are calcu- lated using the durations defined in the routing or master recipe. The time that was actually required for execution is recorded in the production confirmations. Using this app, you can easily compare these values. You can perform a step-by-step analysis for a specific time period and continuously fine-tune the production facility. 21.3 Summary This chapter covered how to leverage some of the next-generation SAP Fiori apps for PP. The information covered in this chapter can certainly help a planner use the same reporting tools and features in other logistics and supply chain components of an SAP S/4HANA system. To conclude, PP in the SAP S/4HANA system is quite comprehensive and offers in- depth and practical solutions for a large number of business processes and scenarios in a diverse range of industries. It also offers options to implement and integrate PP in all important business processes. However, your company can only ensure an actual return on its investment in SAP S/4HANA system implementation by striving to understand the integration of various SAP S/4HANA system areas with each other. You can achieve flawless integration during an SAP S/4HANA system implementation by maintaining extensive collaboration and intense coordination with the various stake- holders of your company. In addition, those who work in supply chain management roles have seen how the PP function is vital to many processes in the logistics and sup- ply chain. © 1065 The Author Jawad Akhtar is an SAP logistics and supply chain management expert with a focus on business sales and delivery.
